Interferometric Imaging

UV Synthesis Builds Images From Sparse Measurements.

Each antenna pair samples one Fourier component of the sky. Grow the array outward and toggle Earth Rotation to watch coverage fill in, sidelobes relax, and the dirty image become easier to interpret.

Idirty = FT−1{V(u,v)·S(u,v)}    PSF = FT−1{S(u,v)}

Sky model: Cygnus A at 5 GHz. PSF colormap: blue = negative sidelobes, dark→white = positive.

What To Look For

Better UV coverage suppresses structured sidelobes first. The dirty image sharpens only after the sampling mask becomes more uniform.

Control Array Size
Toggle Earth Rotation

UV Coverage To Dirty Image To PSF

The four panels stay synchronized so the sampling pattern, beam structure, and reconstruction quality can be read as one story.
